title: Deployment Record
purpose: Record the state of a service deployment so that any agent or human can determine: is it healthy, what was deployed, and how to roll back if something goes wrong. This is the receipt of a deploy — proof that it happened and proof that it's working.
context: You are deploying a service to a container-based platform (Railway, Fly, ECS, etc.). After the deploy completes, verify health and record the result. If any check fails, the status is degraded or failed and the rollback plan becomes the next action. The deployment record is written to the project's deploy log — it's the audit trail.
constraints: All three checks must pass for status 'healthy'. Any failure means 'degraded' or 'failed'.
The rollback section is mandatory — every deploy must have a way back. If migrations aren't reversible, say so explicitly.
The SHA must be a real git commit, not a branch name. The rollback SHA must be the last known-good deploy.
If you cannot verify health (endpoint unreachable, logs inaccessible), status is 'failed' not 'unknown'. Unknown is not a valid status.

sha.description: Git commit SHA that was deployed — not a branch name

environment.description: Where it was deployed. Always deploy to staging first.

status.description: Post-deploy health. Healthy = all checks pass. Degraded = partial. Failed = broken or unreachable.

smoke_test.description: One core operation verified post-deploy (e.g., create a record, send a request)

logs_clean.description: No errors or unhandled exceptions in the first 5 minutes of logs

previous_sha.description: Last known-good commit — verified healthy in a previous deployment record

command.description: Exact command to execute rollback. Copy-paste ready.

migration_reversible.description: Can the database migration be reversed? If false, rollback requires manual DB work.
